---
title: "Assignment 3: SVD"
author: "Nia Steely"
date: today
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: docs
format:
  pdf:
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 12pt
    mainfont: "Times New Roman"
engine: jupyter
jupyter: quarto-env

header-includes: |
  \usepackage{setspace}
  \onehalfspacing

editor: source
---

```{python}
#| label: python setup
#| echo: false

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from PIL import Image
```

```{python}
#| label: step-1
#| echo: false

def svd_blur(image_path, k_values=[5, 20, 50, 100]):
    # Load image and convert to grayscale
    img = Image.open("NiasImage.jpg").convert('L')  # 'L' = grayscale
    A = np.array(img, dtype=np.float64)

    # Apply SVD
    U, S, VT = np.linalg.svd(A, full_matrices=False)

    # Plot original
    plt.figure(figsize=(12, 3))
    plt.subplot(1, len(k_values)+1, 1)
    plt.imshow(A, cmap='gray')
    plt.title('Original')
    plt.axis('off')

    # Reconstruct with different k
    for idx, k in enumerate(k_values, start=2):
        Ak = U[:, :k] @ np.diag(S[:k]) @ VT[:k, :]
        plt.subplot(1, len(k_values)+1, idx)
        plt.imshow(Ak, cmap='gray')
        plt.title(f'k = {k}')
        plt.axis('off')

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

# Usage
svd_blur('building.jpg', k_values=[1, 5, 10, 20, 50])
```

```{python}
#| label: step-2
#| echo: false

# Function to compress a block using SVD
def blockwise_svd(image, k, block_size = 8):

    height, width = image.shape
    compressed = np.zeros_like(image)

    for i in range(0, height, block_size):
        for j in range(0, width, block_size):

            # Extract block
            block = image[i:i+block_size, j:j+block_size]

            # Compress block
            compressed_block = compress_block(block, k)

            # Place back into image
            compressed[i:i+block_size, j:j+block_size] = compressed_block

    return compressed

# Compress a single block using top-k SVD
def compress_block(block, k):

    U, S, VT = np.linalg.svd(block, full_matrices=False)

    # Keep only top k singular values
    U_k = U[:, :k]
    S_k = np.diag(S[:k])
    VT_k = VT[:k, :]

    # Reconstruct block
    reconstructed = U_k @ S_k @ VT_k

    return reconstructed

```

```{python}
#| label: step-3
#| echo: false
#| include: false

# Load image 
img = Image.open("NiasImage.jpg").convert('L')
image = np.array(img, dtype=np.float64)

# Compress and save images for k = 1 to 8
for k in range(1, 9):

    # Apply block SVD compression
    compressed_image = blockwise_svd(image, k, block_size=8)

    # Compute compression ratio
    compression_ratio = 64 / (k * (8 + 8 + 1))

    print(f"k = {k}, Compression Ratio = {compression_ratio:.3f}")

    # Save compressed and reconstructed image for each k
    plt.imsave(f"compressed_k{k}.png", compressed_image, cmap='gray')
```

```{python}
#| label: step-4
#| echo: false

# Plot compression ratio v. k
k_values = np.arange(1, 9)
compression_ratios = 64 / (k_values * (8 + 8 + 1))
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 3))
plt.plot(k_values, compression_ratios, marker='o')
plt.title('Compression Ratio vs. k')
plt.xlabel('k (Number of Singular Values)')
plt.ylabel('Compression Ratio')
plt.xticks(k_values)
plt.grid()
plt.show()

```
